# Improved speech enhancement model
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class SimpleSpeechEnhancer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_fft=512, hop_length=128, win_length=512):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_fft = n_fft
        self.hop_length = hop_length
        self.win_length = win_length
        
        # Convolutional layers for processing spectrograms
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(2, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1)  # Real and imag -> 32 channels
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
        
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
        
        self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.bn3 = nn.BatchNorm2d(128)
        
        self.conv4 = nn.Conv2d(128, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.bn4 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
        
        self.conv5 = nn.Conv2d(64, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.bn5 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
        
        self.conv6 = nn.Conv2d(32, 2, kernel_size=3, padding=1)  # Output real and imag
        
        # Activation functions
        self.relu = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.sigmoid = nn.Sigmoid()
        
    def forward(self, x):
        # x shape: (batch, length)
        batch_size, length = x.shape
        device = x.device
        
        # Apply STFT using PyTorch
        stft_result = torch.stft(x, 
                                n_fft=self.n_fft, 
                                hop_length=self.hop_length, 
                                win_length=self.win_length,
                                window=torch.hann_window(self.win_length).to(device),
                                return_complex=True)
        
        # stft_result shape: (batch, freq_bins, time_frames)
        # Convert to magnitude and phase representation
        magnitude = torch.abs(stft_result)
        phase = torch.angle(stft_result)
        
        # Stack magnitude and phase as channels
        spec_stacked = torch.stack([magnitude, phase], dim=1)  # (batch, 2, freq_bins, time_frames)
        
        # Process through convolutional layers
        out = self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(spec_stacked)))  # (batch, 32, freq_bins, time_frames)
        out = self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(out)))  # (batch, 64, freq_bins, time_frames)
        out = self.relu(self.bn3(self.conv3(out)))  # (batch, 128, freq_bins, time_frames)
        out = self.relu(self.bn4(self.conv4(out)))  # (batch, 64, freq_bins, time_frames)
        out = self.relu(self.bn5(self.conv5(out)))  # (batch, 32, freq_bins, time_frames)
        out = self.conv6(out)  # (batch, 2, freq_bins, time_frames)
        
        # Add residual connection
        out = out + spec_stacked
        
        # Separate magnitude and phase
        enhanced_magnitude = out[:, 0, :, :]  # (batch, freq_bins, time_frames)
        enhanced_phase = out[:, 1, :, :]     # (batch, freq_bins, time_frames)
        
        # Ensure magnitude is positive
        enhanced_magnitude = torch.clamp(enhanced_magnitude, min=0)
        
        # Combine magnitude and phase to form complex spectrogram
        enhanced_real = enhanced_magnitude * torch.cos(enhanced_phase)
        enhanced_imag = enhanced_magnitude * torch.sin(enhanced_phase)
        enhanced_spec = torch.complex(enhanced_real, enhanced_imag)
        
        # Apply inverse STFT
        enhanced_audio = torch.istft(enhanced_spec, 
                                    n_fft=self.n_fft, 
                                    hop_length=self.hop_length, 
                                    win_length=self.win_length,
                                    window=torch.hann_window(self.win_length).to(device))
        
        # Ensure output has same length as input
        if enhanced_audio.shape[1] != length:
            if enhanced_audio.shape[1] > length:
                enhanced_audio = enhanced_audio[:, :length]
            else:
                # Pad with zeros if shorter
                pad_len = length - enhanced_audio.shape[1]
                enhanced_audio = F.pad(enhanced_audio, (0, pad_len))
        
        return enhanced_audio
